title: Millbrook Herald: City Water Tests Show Slightly Elevated Lead
snippet: Millbrook water tests show lead at 18 ppb, slightly above the EPA action level of 15 ppb.
content: Millbrook's municipal water supply has tested at 18 parts per billion for lead, slightly above the Environmental Protection Agency's action level of 15 ppb, according to a report released yesterday. The city's Water Authority director, James Thornton, said the results require the city to optimize its corrosion control treatment but stressed that 'this is a marginal exceedance, not a crisis.' The EPA report noted that no immediate health advisory was necessary. Thornton said the city would flush hydrants in the oldest sections of the distribution system and offer free lead testing kits to residents in homes built before 1986, which are more likely to have lead service lines. The follow-up testing is scheduled for August.
